programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Transition de genre à l'adolescence : est-ce aux parents d'avoir le dernier mot?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Alex Perron, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Me Nada Boumeftah et notre invitée la créatrice de contenu Gabrielle Marion.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre un programme national d'alimentation scolaire?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Antonine Yaccarini, Rodolphe Husny et notre invitée la chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Palmarès des pires routes du Québec : est-ce possible d'avoir un réseau routier en bon état?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et notre invité l'ancien ministre libéral Robert Poëti.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre une taxe touristique pour visiter certaines régions du Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Déborah Cherenfant et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Augmentation du trafic à Montréal-Trudeau : doit-on investir dans un autre aéroport?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Maude Goyer, Victor Henriquez, Déborah Cherenfant et notre invité le spécialiste de l'aviation Mehran Ebrahimi.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on abolir la taxe de bienvenue pour les premiers acheteurs?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et notre invité le courtier immobilier Younes El Moustir.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre publier des photos de ses enfants sur les réseaux sociaux?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Dr Alain Vadeboncoeur, Victor Henriquez et notre invitée l'avocate Mme Marie-Elaine Tremblay.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Doit-on rendre possible le vote par internet pour nos différentes élections?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Rodolphe Husny, Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez et Valérie Beaudoin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Budget fédéral : le déficit de 40 G$ est-il justifié?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Rodolphe Husny et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre des matchs de saison régulière du Canadien à Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Catherine Beauchamp et notre invité le spécialiste sports et affaires Ray Lalonde.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Sécurité dans le métro : est-ce que l'ajout de constables spéciaux est la solution?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Geneviève Pettersen, Maude Goyer et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Est-ce qu'on vit une crise de la masculinité?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Geneviève Pettersen, Maude Goyer et notre invité, l'humoriste Louis T.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Me Anne-France Goldwater et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on imposer des politiques de diversité aux entreprises privées?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Catherine Beauchamp, Antonine Yaccarini, Me Nada Boumeftah et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Le gouvernement du Québec devrait-il gérer tout le transport collectif?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Rodolphe Husny, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Victor Henriquez et Justine McIntyre.

programme.title: Magic in the Moonlight
programme.desc: In this romantic comedy, a British skeptic (Colin Firth, "A Single Man") falls in love with a spiritualist medium (Emma Stone, "Easy A") whom he hoped to debunk.

programme.title: My Mom's New Boyfriend
programme.desc: Henry Durand (Colin Hanks) is a young FBI agent who is given a difficult assignment: spy on his mother (Meg Ryan) and her boyfriend (Antonio Banderas) who is suspected of leading a gang of art thieves.

programme.title: A Christmas Karen
programme.desc: After displaying her privilege, entitled middle-aged woman Karen receives an intervention by some spirits.

programme.title: The Long Walk Home
programme.desc: One black and one white woman in 1955 Montgomery, Alabama must decide what they are going to do in response to the famous bus boycott lead by Martin Luther King.

programme.title: Quicksand
programme.desc: Banker Martin Raikes (Michael Keaton) heads to Monaco to investigate strange financial activity surrounding a film production that his bank is financing. There he encounters the film's star, the washed-up, hard-drinking one-time action star Jake Mellows (Michael Caine). Before he can conduct a thorough investigation, Raikes finds himself framed for the assassination of a government official. With both crooks and the law out to get him, Raikes goes on the run, eventually hooking up with Mellows.

programme.title: K-19: The Widowmaker
programme.desc: Follows Captain Alexi Vostrikov (Harrison Ford) who, at the height of the Cold War, is ordered to take over command of the nuclear missile submarine K-19, pride of the Soviet Navy. His assignment: Prepare the K-19 for sea and take her out on patrol -- no matter what the cost. But problems with the K-19 arise that may lead to a core meltdown and explosion that will certainly kill all aboard, and possibly trigger a nuclear war. Vostrikov must choose between his orders and the lives of his men.

When a young unicorn (Mia Farrow) learns that she's in danger and that she may soon be the last of her kind, she leaves the safety of her protected forest and enlists the help of Schmendrick (Alan Arkin), a gentle, albeit clumsy, sorcerer. Together, they embark on a long and dangerous journey with one goal: to defeat Haggard and save the unicorns from extinction.

programme.title: Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer

Then she finds out that Rocky is off to tame lions and that Amy is flying to Borneo with her mother to save a lost tribe. Judy is bummed to be left home with her rotten brother, Stink (Parris Mosteller), and her second-best friend, Frank Pearl. programme.title: Best Mistake 1 : The Movie
programme.desc: Kim Yeon Do (Lee Eun Jae) is a studious high school girl whose life is being made miserable by a boy from her past. Despite all of her efforts, she can't get peace as he won't leave her alone. So she hatches a plan. On her social media pages, she posts a picture of another boy, in the hope that this move will put an end to the irritation. But this move turns out to have unexpected consequences: It turns out that the boy in the picture is actually one of her schoolmates. Named Ji Hyun Ho (Kang Yul), he is a bit of a loose cannon. Although he is very popular, he dislikes studying - and prefers play. The rest of the school soon learns about the social media post, as news spreads fast in the classroom! This leads to a number of run-ins with ji Hyun Ho. But the more they get to know one another, the closer they get - causing yet further complications for a whole host of classmates!

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles

programme.title: Buffalo Keepers
programme.desc: Buffalo Keepers - Buffalo Keepers: Today, the buffalo is the quintessential symbol of strength, freedom, and the unspoiled American West. But in the late 1800s, the nation's buffalo were nearly wiped out by ruthless hide hunters. Those majestic herds, which once numbered in the tens of millions, were down to less than 500 animals. This is the story of the handful of determined men and women who mobilized to save the ones that were left in a last-ditch effort to stave off extinction. Ranchers like Charles and Mary Ann Goodnight rescued orphaned buffalo calves and built small herds. Former buffalo hunters like Buffalo Bill helped build public sympathy. And politicians like Theodore Roosevelt helped pass laws to protect the remaining animals. The effort would be a generational one, with buffalo herds carefully tended, bred, and passed down to new stewards when their old protectors passed away. Thanks to their efforts, there are an estimated 500,000 buffalo thundering across America's grasslands once again.

programme.title: Wagon Train

And finally, Matt and Carlos go head to head against a boxing manager and his fight fixing fighter.

programme.title: Lou's Big Gamble
programme.desc: It's a brand new start for the Operation Repo team when Lou bets big on a high-risk opportunity and sets up shop in Las Vegas! But first, Ronnie and Froy must wrangle a wrestling ring from an lucha libre school, and Carlos and Matt have to break up a Botox party when they go after a car owned by a upper class housewife. Then on their first repo in Vegas, a live tiger stands between the team and a truck, and it seems like Vegas is more than they can handle. If they complete their mission, the payout could be enormous, but will they be able to resist the temptations of ...

programme.title: The Showstopper
programme.desc: While attempting a repo of a "green energy" bio-diesel vehicle, Froy's (Froylan Tercero) lack of knowledge of the vehicle's specialized fuel system sets the bus ablaze... and when his error causes it to explode, he and Matt (Matt Burch) abandon builders/owners Mickie (Michael Q. Schmidt) and Joey (Gino Calabro) in the middle of the desert.

programme.title: Cement Mixer

programme.title: Think, Adapt and Attack
programme.desc: When a debtor and his friends resort to baseball bats, chaos erupts, and Lou, Lyndah and Sonia fight not only with them, but also with each other. Down in Texas, Matt and Froy wait to clear up Froy's warrant and get in way over their heads working for a local repo team. Meanwhile, Lou decides to re-hire Frankie despite Sonia and Lyndah's objections, but first Lou wants to see how Frankie fares when faced with the temptation in the form of strippers and alcohol.

programme.title: Tactical Planning
programme.desc: Matt and Froy walk into a moral gray area when they repossess a vehicle by stealing it. Then, Sonia and Matt discover their target van is being used in an investigation of highly toxic material. And, Matt poses as a valet to retrieve a car from an irate diner and his befuddled date.

programme.title: Super-Sonia

Froy and Sonia wind up in the middle of a relationship dispute with an absent-minded professor who's got too many cars and too many girlfriends, Matt and Froy confront a debtor with a tiny motorcycle and an enormous mean streak, and Sonia and Lyndah find themselves in the middle of a gang dispute gone horribly awry.

programme.title: Breakdowns and Beatdowns
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy head into a bad part of town to find that their target car is owned by bat-equipped gang bangers. programme.title: The Country House Revealed
programme.desc: In a six-part series, architectural historian Dan Cruickshank visits houses in Britain never before open to public view, including South Wraxall Manor, Kinross House in Scotland, Easton Neston, Wentworth Woodhouse, Clandeboye in Northern Ireland, and Marshcourt. Cruickshank roams the corridors and rummages in the cellars as he tells the story of each house -- who built them, the generations who lived in them, and the families who lost them.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: As the prime cuts are often the first to go, most families are left with a freezer brimming with ground wild game meat. Pulled from the pages of his forthcoming Wild Game Cookbook, Steve shares a variety of recipes from burger to meatloaf and more ‚Äì designed to whittle down this supply and jazz up your ground meat game.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 1
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America. In this three-part series, he travels deep into the Amazon jungle to hunt with and learn from the locals there. Not only will this be one heck of a hunting adventure, it will be a mind-opening, life-changing event. From bowfishing to hunting in the jungle at night, you never know what the evening's menu will bring.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 2
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America. In this three-part series, he travels deep into the Amazon jungle to hunt with and learn from the locals there. Not only will this be one heck of a hunting adventure, it will be a mind-opening, life-changing event. From bowfishing to hunting in the jungle at night, you never know what the evening's menu will bring.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 3
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America. In this three-part series, he travels deep into the Amazon jungle to hunt with and learn from the locals there. Not only will this be one heck of a hunting adventure, it will be a mind-opening, life-changing event. From bowfishing to hunting in the jungle at night, you never know what the evening's menu will bring.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: About Time: Helen and Brittany Hunt Elk in Montana
programme.desc: Brittany and Helen have put more work in on MeatEater than just about anyone else, but have yet to hunt a big game animal. Steven Rinella takes them through the process from beginning to end. They need to get their hunters safety, learn to shoot, train for the woods, and get geared up. When November comes they're ready to go, and they strike out into the mountains of northern Montana for the adventure of their lives.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Sky Island Solitaire: Backpack Hunting Coues Deer in Arizona
programme.desc: Steve returns to southern Arizona to stalk the elusive Coues deer. Unaccompanied, Steve immerses himself in the quiet southwest desert and gets intimate with one of the most wary animals in the west. Throughout the hunt, Steve articulates why solo time in trophy country is good for the soul.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Ribeye of the Sky: Sandhill Cranes in West Texas
programme.desc: Steve and bird hunting aficionado Ronny Boehme join up with wildlife biologist Ed Arnett in Lubbock, Texas to hunt Sandhill cranes. The Sandhill crane offers a unique wingshooting opportunity and also carries the nickname "rib eye of the sky" because of its supposed similarities to a handsome cut of beef. ¬†It's fast action and plenty of laughs as Steve, Ronny, and Ed explore the culinary delights of these birds with a Texas style "Crane Cookout."

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: The Coldest Hunt: Nunivak Island Muskox
programme.desc: Lightning strikes twice: Steven Rinella has somehow beaten the odds and drawn a second Muskox tag after having to forfeit his first one four years ago due to unforeseen circumstances. Steve has always regretted passing up an opportunity to hunt Muskox but with another tag in his pocket, nothing will stop this adventure. We learn about the native history and culture of Nunivak Island's as Steve tries to stay warm while chasing Muskox 30 miles offshore in the Bering Sea.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: The last time Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen went out with Steven Rinella they were soaked head to toe and came home meatless. To remedy their meat crisis while keeping them dry, Steve sets up a springtime wild turkey hunt in sunny California. Steve gives Joe and Bryan the A to Z on hunting, butchering, and cooking the wild turkey. Steve caps off the introductory lesson by preparing his favorite turkey recipe: Schnitzel.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: In the first part of this series, Steve attempts a unique spring hunt for sooty grouse in the coastal rainforests of southeast Alaska. These mysterious hooters make for a hunt that is markedly different from any other and success doesn't come easily. They say blind squirrels can find a nut, but will Steve harvest a grouse?

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: In Part One, Steve experienced the difficulty of hunting the Sooty grouse of southeast Alaska. In Part 2, Steve joins his friend Barbara in Juneau who shows him the in and outs of finding "hooters." Tromping through Southeast Alaska rainforest, Steve and Barbara discover a mutual fondness for an unconventional hunt. Barbara concludes the hunt with a beachside meal of fettuccine a la sooty grouse.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Wild to Table: Memorable Meals Cooking Special
programme.desc: Steve loves to fish as much as he loves to hunt.¬†Steve is on a river in SW Montana for a day to catch some rainbow trout and cook a few over a fire. As fishing and campfire cooking offer time for reflection, Steve takes the time to recall a few memorable MeatEater meals.¬†From caveman style sheep ribs in the Alaska Range to curiosity-induced coyote BBQ in Mexico, these meals give Steve the chance to explore what it means to be a MeatEater.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Up at the Cabin: Prince of Wales Island Black Bear
programme.desc: Last spring, Steve decided to pass up an opportunity to kill a black bear in favor of an extended up close and personal experience. This time, Steve heads back to his shack on Prince of Wales Island and he's brought his friend Paul Neess from Vortex Optics along to share the experience of using a canoe to slip in close to these giant black bears.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: All across America hunters kick off the fall hunting season with a weekend of doves, friends, and food. Steve's lifelong friend, Ronny Boehme has invited Steve out to his digs in Virginia for the dove opener. To prep for opening day, Steve and Ron spend a day brushing up on their shooting skills on a sporting clays course followed by a pigeon hunt. Steve shares simple, delicious cooking methods for doves and pigeons.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Ryan Callaghan and Aaron Evans join Steve on an epic adventure in British Columbia's Rockies to pursue grizzly bears. Steve has long desired to take a grizzly but only if he can do it in a way that fits his personal ethics. The hunt is dampened by frustrating weather but Steve finds a way to fix a tasty meal.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry has big weekend plans for a fantasy baseball draft when he's told he has to cover Saturday detention. He ends up monitoring a motley crew of Xavier students while trying to make an early escape to salvage his fantasy picks before he misses the draft. In an effort to bury the hatchet and come to terms with Lisa's appointment as the new principal, Robert and Lisa have an impromptu heart to heart.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry's been given another coaching assignment, but this time he's helping Ricki prepare for a fencing tournament, and he couldn't be less interested. After an uncharacteristic heart to heart, however, Gerry changes his tunes and assures Ricki that he'll get her to the tournament – a task that proves more difficult than he had anticipated. In an effort to capitalize on Dwyer's recent divorce, Bobbi invites him over for dinner and attempts to seduce him the only way she knows how. Poorly. Robert, meanwhile, is consumed with uncovering his lunch thief.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: When he attempts to reign in a bratty student, Gerry is faced with the wrath of his notoriously overprotective and obnoxious mother. After an innocent interaction with her takes an unpleasant and threatening turn, Gerry goes to great lengths to protect his reputation. Upon realizing that she has fallen into the role of staff “punching bag,” Bobbi attempts to transfer the title onto another staff member, but her awkward and cruel efforts only further seal her title. Meanwhile, Ricki sells pencils to Robert and drives a hard bargain.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry accompanies students on a leadership retreat to a nature centre where he meets the beautiful staff member, Jasmine. As Gerry and Robert engage in a misguided competition to win her affection, the two find themselves lost in the woods and in pretty dire straights. Meanwhile, when Lisa finds out that Trudy is running a morbid “death pool” gambling ring, she attempts to shut it down, only to learn that she's the odds on favourite to die next. And Simon and Frank's innocent game of rugby spoons takes an ugly turn.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is turning forty, and after finding a letter he wrote to himself ten years earlier, he becomes acutely aware of everything he has failed to accomplish. Clearly not the family man, Gerry attempts to prove that he's the other type of “awesome forty.” But when his wild night out doesn't go as planned, Gerry takes drastic measures to meet the expectations set out in his own letter. Meanwhile, Simon's ferret goes missing and Lisa wants to impress the chairman of the board of trustees by putting on an elaborate fun fair in his honour, which quickly devolves into a complete disaster.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: After waking up married and quite hung-over, Gerry and Lisa do their best to face the workday. They quickly realize, however, that this ‘accidental marriage' thing is getting in the way and they set off to get an annulment as soon as possible. Meanwhile, Nisha accepts an invitation to have dinner with Simon and his mother in the hopes of mending fences. But when Angela's date shows up, bearing an uncanny resemblance to Simon, it becomes clear that Angela's acceptance of Nisha doesn't come without strings. Ms. Terdie feels redundant when she learns that Robert has ordered e-readers for the students.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: As Gerry begins the process of moving in to Lisa's apartment, Lisa finds herself deeply regretting her decision to give the marriage a shot. Meanwhile, in an effort to get in shape and lower his stress levels, Robert gets himself a treadmill desk. Trudy explains that Robert's new attire combined with the full-body sweat and disturbing ‘workout' noises emanating from his office are unprofessional and disruptive, but her words land on deaf ears. Bobbi takes great pride in learning that she has inspired a former student to become a singer/songwriter, but she soon realizes her pride might be misplaced.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry sees dollar signs when he learns that young Hayden's father is sick, very rich, and planning to leave something to Hayden's favourite staff members. Already established as Hayden's Number One teacher, Gerry awaits his monetary gift with eager anticipation, only to discover that his ‘gift' is legal guardianship of Hayden. But Gerry's initial disappointment is quickly remedied by the financial contribution that accompanies said guardianship. Unfortunately, raising a child turns out to be a tad more expensive than Gerry had anticipated. Meanwhile, Robert becomes co-principal and takes every opportunity to ensure that all staff and students understand that he is no longer Lisa's subordinate. Nisha jumps in on some innocent student games during recess, only to injure herself and ruin everything.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: It's almost March break and all of the staff members seem to have saved enough money for a vacation, except for Gerry. In a desperate effort to make some fast cash for a trip of his own, he decides to take a side job teaching in a prison. Gerry manages to establish a surprisingly positive rapport with the inmates, only to find himself the accidental drug mule for one of his new students. Meanwhile, Simon has been teaching an ESL class and becomes convinced one of his students has a much better grasp of the English language than he's been letting on. Nisha has been tutoring students at their homes, but finds the work environments difficult to navigate.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: After one of Gerry's students skips a detention for a theatre rehearsal, Gerry confronts the student mid-rehearsal and unleashes a barrage of vitriol that impresses director Emma so much so that she immediately fires the student, offering Gerry the starring role instead. Will this be the birth of a brilliant thespian? In an effort to motivate her lazy students, Bobbi equips them all with heart monitors. At first, her efforts appear to be successful, but Bobbi soon finds herself unintentionally tracking a different type of student exercise. Nisha's been having trouble getting her class to respect her authority, but has figured out that she can manipulate them into paying attention by crying in the classroom. Robert cracks down on Nisha's unconventional tactic, only to find himself taking a page out of her book.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry volunteers to cover Simon's girls' volleyball team, assuring Bobbi that he won't ruin their playoff run. But when Bill reminds him of their friend's wedding on Saturday, Gerry's afraid he'll miss his shot with Suzie the bridesmaid. He sets out to do the only right thing: throw the first game, thereby freeing up his Saturday. Meanwhile, staff and students are finding they don't have enough time between classes to get all the way across the school, so Robert devises a plan to get everybody moving through the hallways more quickly. When Trudy learns she has an allergy to soy nuts, Lisa insists she provide a photo for Xavier's Allergy Wall, in the event of an emergency.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is turning forty, and after finding a letter he wrote to himself ten years earlier, he becomes acutely aware of everything he has failed to accomplish. programme.title: Baby Einstein

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Don't Forget the Leaves!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends investigate and discover that leaves are food for animals, they provide shade, and they help make nutrients so flowers and trees can grow!

programme.title: The Bug Club
programme.desc: Sid and his friends use their magnifying glasses and discover ants, rolie polies, and caterpillars around the playground.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - The Rescue / Shaq's Secret Birthday: Zoo keepers at the Orlanta Zoo ask for the Paq's help to transport and release two turtles into their natural habitat, Crystal Lake. Gronk and Tibbs set out after picking up the turtles, but Gronk's carelessness ends up putting the turtles in danger. Tibbs and Gronk must figure out a way to save the turtles and get the home successfully. / When the Paq decided to throw Shaq a surprise part for his birthday, they fan out over Orlanta in an attempt to get his favorite food, singer, and cake. Unfortunately nothing goes right and the Paq has to scramble to fix everything before Shaq comes home.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - The Secret of Rockyhenge / The Case of The Crimson Car: Ranger Raoul accidentally knows over one of the giant stones at Rockyhenge. After he calls the Paq in to help lift it back up into place, Biggie D, Gronk, and Himari inadvertently make it worse. They have to scramble to put everything back into place before the Head Ranger shows up. / After Miguel's beloved car is stolen, the Paq is tasked with finding out who did it. Dawkins and Tibbs are on the case, but they have no idea the notorious thief James Moriarty is behind the theft. Dawkins and Tibbs must use every investigative skill they have to the test to find the criminal and the stolen car.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Santa Pac: Betrayus plans to put an end to PacWorld's most festive holiday by getting rid of the holiday's icon - Santa Pac.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Pac's Very Scary Halloween - Part One: Forever unable to come up with a creature powerful enough to vanquish Pac-Man, Betrayus employs the diabolical Dr. Pacenstein (who is now a scheming brain-in-a-jar) to create a mega monster.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Peek-a-Boo Beluga: When a beluga whale gets lost and ends up in New England, Preston wants to keep her and won't let the Rangers take her home. The Rangers must stop Priscilla's cheaply made solar-paneled drones which have gone haywire before they overcharge and explode.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Tiny Rocket Man: On a quest to figure out how to get back to Kaleidoscopia, Preston sends up a space shuttle piloted by a tiny robot Preston, which loses communication with Earth due to overcrowding of the communication spectrum. / When an algae superbloom stops seagrass from growing in Florida waters, the Rangers must help a hungry manatee and find a solution to this smelly problem.

programme.title: Angry Birds

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Talking Ben invents a comedy robot! But the laughter stops when the robot decides to bring down Tom and Ben Enterprises.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: The Landlord hustles the gang for control of Tom and Ben Enterprises.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - The Search for Geogan! / The Geogan Arcleon!: In search of the mysterious Geogan they witnessed the night before, the AB have a run in with Crystal Blue, Everett and Strata. / An AAAnimus Security Force transport vehicle loaded with confiscated Baku-Balls is hijacked.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - Chase the Mysterious Bakugan! / Drago in the Crosshairs!: There are reports all over Los Volmos of a rogue Bakugan rampaging at night and destroying people's property. / A strange golden Bakugan has been appearing at practise fields across Los Volmos in search of Dragonoid of Pyrule.

programme.title: Superhero Kindergarten
programme.desc: Superhero Kindergarten - The Experiment: Billy and Jackson get into a tricky situation when Nigel Danforth hatches a plan to get his evil superpowers back.

programme.title: Friends Reunite
programme.desc: Olly about how his obsession is affecting their relationships. Olly refuses to see the damage he's causing, but it becomes clear that he's lost touch with what really matters.

programme.title: All Of Nothing
programme.desc: The ninja and Sensei Wu try to infiltrate the Serpentines' inner sanctum and walk right into a trap, and when Lloyd's rescue mission fails, there is only one person who can help them, namely the evil Lord Garmadon – but can they trust him?

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes
programme.desc: Spider-Man and Marvel's Super Heroes take on a mischievous Loki and a team of super villains in an all-new LEGO adventure

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: The CHI Jackers
programme.desc: The Wolves have become strong because of the stolen CHI. And now they are stealing even more. Soon all the other tribes of CHIMA have none. Laval and Eris must find a new, clever and “Wolf proof ” way to get CHI to the tribes.

programme.title: Symptoms!
programme.desc: Owen falls prey to the toxin of a poisonous prehistoric jungle flower and believes he's a raptor.

programme.title: Snow Way
programme.desc: Practicing for the local snow lodge's big snowboard race, Mia has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no biggie, but when she tries to resume practice, the normally fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! At the local snow lodge's Snowboard Meet, ace snowboarder Mia, trying to raise money for a new Heartlake City animal shelter, enters the fiercest race of all – the Boardercross. Then, while practicing, she has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no big deal, but once she heals and tries to resume practice, the usually fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! With the help of her Friends, she tries hard to overcome her fear, but her nightmarish visions of snowboard monsters and jeering snowmen have her shaking in her bed. She backs out of the race, disappointing her Friends and herself. Finally, when an avalanche traps Fiona, Mia is forced to overcome her fears and race to the rescue.

programme.title: Holiday Gift Swap Disaster
programme.desc: While the rest of the group gets creative and makes new, personalized gifts from scratch. In the end, it's a reminder that it's not about the gifts—it's about the thought and effort you put into showing you care.

programme.title: Day Of The Great Devourer
programme.desc: Disaster threatens as the ninja must fight to defeat the all consuming Great Devourer in a desperate attempt to save Ninjago.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes
programme.desc: The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. While preparing for a party at Avengers' Tower, THE AVENGERS (IRON MAN, CAPTAIN AMERICA, BLACK WIDOW, HAWKEYE, THOR, HULK and VISION) notice Iron Man acting strangely. When Iron Man volunteers to go out and get more ice, they find it odd: when has Tony every volunteered to get anything? Suddenly, ULTRON appears on a screen: he's taken control of Iron Man! To make matters worse, Ultron takes over all the versions of Iron Man's armor and they fly off! The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN! Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: RIIZE Refreshing Tracks Playlist
programme.desc: Uplifting beats and youthful charm — RIIZE's refreshing moments in music.

programme.title: Hearts2Hearts 'FOCUS' Trailer
programme.desc: Hearts2Hearts' first mini album FOCUS features a total of six tracks, including the title track “FOCUS,” the single “STYLE,” and the pre-release song “Pretty Please,” showcasing a diverse musical palette. The album trailer captures the members' bold and free-spirited sides as they play around and dance together in a quiet space. In the latter part of the video, the title track “FOCUS” audio and part of the choreography are revealed, raising anticipation for the new release.

programme.title: Hearts2Hearts 'FOCUS' MV
programme.desc: Hearts2Hearts' first mini album title track “FOCUS” is a house genre-based song that features a vintage piano riff and an addictive melody, showcasing the group's cool and chic vocal charm. Choreographed by Jo Na-In, the performance delivers a sleek and sophisticated atmosphere.Set against a school backdrop, the music video delicately captures the complex emotions between the members. Its lyrics sensually express the feeling of being completely focused on someone, offering a fresh glimpse into Hearts2Hearts' unique appeal.

programme.title: NCT DREAM : They Were Here To Beat It Up Trailer
programme.desc: Centering on the theme of “the speed of time,” the album delivers a bold message that the members — who have been running toward their dreams since childhood — continue to grow, while confidently moving forward at their own pace and in their own way. NCT DREAM's 6th mini album “Beat It Up” consists of six tracks, including the title track of the same name, “Beat It Up.” The trailer “They Were Here To Beat It Up” wittily portrays NCT DREAM breaking through their limits and opening a new chapter of their own stage with surreal visuals and storytelling, powerfully setting the tone for the beginning of the new album.

programme.title: NCT DREAM 'Beat It Up' MV
programme.desc: The title track “Beat It Up” hits hard with bold kicks, heavy bass, and a vibe that's all about breaking limits with confidence. NCT DREAM's sixth mini album “Beat It Up” takes on the theme of 'the speed of time', sending a message that they're moving forward in their own pace and style. The music video amps it up even more with a boxer concept, powerful moves, snappy cuts, and punch-heavy choreography that fires off the song's energy.

programme.title: RIIZE 'Fame' Trailer
programme.desc: RIIZE's new single “Fame” is set to be released. all captured through RIIZE's unique genre of “emotional pop.”It expresses the members' moments of anxiety, emptiness, and the intense emotions that arise amid their struggles, Focusing on the unseen side of RIIZE's growth journey, the single features a total of three tracks, including the title track “Fame.”

programme.title: RIIZE 'Fame' MV
programme.desc: The bold, forward-driving rhythmic structure combined with the raw texture of electric guitar creates a dynamic surge of energy. The lyrics reflect RIIZE's artistic vision, delivering the message that what they truly seek is not fame, but the sharing of emotions and love.RIIZE's second single “Fame” is the group's first venture into a rage-style hip-hop track, expressing their artistic ideals as “emotional pop artists.”

programme.title: JUNGWOO 'SUGAR' MV
programme.desc: NCT JUNGWOO's first solo single “SUGAR” is a groovy pop song with swing‑infused drums, a witty bassline, and a smooth, catchy melody. The lyrics send a warm message of gratitude to fans, wishing that past memories and future moments are all sweet. The music video brings this sweetness to life with colorful, sugar-inspired visuals, while the performance highlights JUNGWOO's stylish movements and precise choreography, showcasing his unique stage presence.

programme.title: TAEYEON 'Panorama' MV
programme.desc: TAEYEON celebrates the 10th anniversary of her solo debut with the compilation album Panorama, featuring the Pop Rock title track “인사 (Panorama).” The song pairs vibrant piano, synths, and a strong drum line with soaring guitar riffs and TAEYEON's delicate yet powerful vocals. The music video presents a cinematic story of TAEYEON, who has lost her memory, and a girl who helps restore it — a metaphor for the bond between the artist and her fans, SONEs. Its emotional, road-movie-style visuals reveal SONEs as the hidden protagonist. The 24-track album offers a retrospective of her past decade, unfolding like a panorama of her solo journey.

programme.title: Miles Davis
programme.desc: American trumpeter Miles Davis (1926-1991) was a pioneering jazz musician from the late 1940s to the late 1980s. His influence on 20th century music is hard to overstate, as he trailblazed bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music. On October 11, 1964, Miles Davis performed at Teatro Dell'Arte in Milan. With tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, Davis performed Autumn Leaves, My Funny Valentine, All Blues, All of You, and Joshua.

programme.title: John Coltrane
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie, Tony Coe, Derek Humble & Billy Mitchell
programme.desc: American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Dizzy Gillespie (1917-1993) was one of the seminal figures of the bebop movement. He fuses all musical forms rooted in African culture, such as music from Cuba, Latin America and the Caribbean, into his music. On November 4, 1970 he played a concert in Denmark with the Kenny Clarke/Francy Boland Big Band, performing Con Alma, Brother K, Now Hear My Meanin', Manteca, Let Me Outta Here, and Things Are Here.

programme.title: Charlie Haden
programme.sub-title: Charlie Haden & Orchestra - North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1985, American jazz bassist and composer Charlie Haden performed a mesmerizing concert at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. Known for his collaborations with free-jazz pioneer Ornette Coleman, Haden brought his own New Liberation Orchestra to the festival.

programme.title: Jaco Pastorius
programme.desc: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a true musical innovator hit the stage: Jaco Pastorius, who transformed the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument. Combining intricate harmonics, fluid melodies, and unparalleled technical skill, Pastorius left a lasting impact on the music world. In this concert, he is accompanied by Peter Erskine (drums), Don Alias (percussion), Othello Molineaux (steel drums), Bob Mintzer (tenor sax and bass clarinet), and Randy Brecker (trumpet and electronics). They perform “Chicken”, “Donna Lee”, “Mr. Phone Bone”, and “Fannie Mae”. Don't miss this extraordinary opportunity to experience Jaco Pastorius's electrifying performance at the Montreal Jazz Festival 1982!

programme.title: Art Farmer
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Earl Hines
programme.desc: Despite his advanced age, the legendary jazz pianist Earl Hines was at the top of his game when he performed in the Belgian town of Spa in 1977. With a band consisting of Rudy Rutherford (reeds), Jimmy Leary (bass), and Eddie Graham (drums), Hines livens up the room with his interpretations of “I Can't Believe That You're In Love With Me” and “The Man I Love.” Marva Josie joins the group for soulful renditions of “A Sunday Kind of Love” and the well-known classic “Kansas City” before the set comes to a close with “Caravan,” which features a lengthy drum solo by Graham.

programme.title: Al McKay & Earth Wind & Fire Experience
programme.sub-title: Al McKay's Earth, Wind & Fire Experience
programme.desc: Every year since 1970, the German city of Burghausen has been hosting one of the largest jazz festivals in the world. During Burghausen International Jazz Week, Burghausen becomes a 'Bavarian jazz mecca': guests from all over the world join the Burghausers to enjoy the most wonderful jazz performances as the colorful hustle and bustle of spectators and musicians shake up the city. One of the bands performing here in 2019 is The Earth, Wind & Fire Experience. In this concert, original Earth, Wind & Fire guitarist, writer and musical director Al McKay performs the band's timeless disco and soul. Together with seven of the thirteen original Earth, Wind & Fire members, he keep hits such as Boogie Wonderland and September alive, touring all over the world.

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie, Tony Coe, Derek Humble & Billy Mitchell
programme.desc: American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Dizzy Gillespie (1917-1993) was one of the seminal figures of the bebop movement. He fuses all musical forms rooted in African culture, such as music from Cuba, Latin America and the Caribbean, into his music. On November 4, 1970 he played a concert in Denmark with the Kenny Clarke/Francy Boland Big Band, performing Con Alma, Brother K, Now Hear My Meanin', Manteca, Let Me Outta Here, and Things Are Here.

programme.title: Aretha Franklin
programme.sub-title: Aretha Franklin: A trip in Paris
programme.desc: Live Recording at the Palais Des Sports from 1977. Aretha Franklin performs songs like ‘Respect', ‘You make me feel like a natural woman', ‘La vie en Rose' and ‘Singing in the Rain'.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester, Andris Nelsons & Baiba Skride
programme.desc: In May 2019, the new principal conductor of the Gewandhaus, Andris Nelsons, presented Tchaikovsky's Symphony No. 5 with his orchestra in combination with Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1 with outstanding violinist Baiba Skride as the soloist. In this composition, the violin does not allow itself a break, continually tells its dark story and gets into a vicious circle of ostinato Passacaglia bass lines again and again falls into beguilingly beautiful singing. Tchaikovsky initially thought his 'Symphony of Fate' was a failure and believed himself to be at the end of his creative powers. It was probably the composer's nature, plagued by self-doubt, that made it almost impossible for him to develop a self-confident attitude to his own creative power. Between this two works, Skride performs Stravinsky's Elegy for Solo Violin.

programme.title: Iván Fischer & Orchestra del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino
programme.sub-title: Mozart Symphony No. 34 & Dvořák Symphony No. 7
programme.desc: Maestro Iván Fischer leads the Orchestra del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino in a remarkable concert program of works by Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart and Antonín Dvořák. Mozart's Symphony No. 34 in C major, K. 338 opens the program. Completed in the summer of 1780, this was the last symphony Mozart wrote in Salzburg, where he worked as a court musician. The three-movement symphony features two vibrant outer movements with fanfares and rousing themes, while the quieter second movement is scored for strings alone, deviating from the typical four-movement structure of the time. Next on the program is Dvořák's Symphony No. 7 in D minor, Op. 70, completed in March 1885 and premiered one month later in London conducted by the composer himself. With its dramatic and dark style, this symphony stands in stark contrast to the predominantly optimistic tone of Dvořák's broader oeuvre. This performance was recorded at Teatro del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino in Florence, Italy, on January 29, 2021.

programme.title: Sir John Eliot Gardiner
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Daniel Harding & Orchestra del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino
programme.sub-title: Brahms Tragic Overture & Mahler Symphony No. 1
programme.desc: The Maggio Musicale Fiorentino (Florence Musical May) is Italy's oldest opera and arts festival. It was founded in 1933 with the aim of presenting contemporary and forgotten operas. Over the years, the scope widened, and orchestral concerts have long been an integral part of the festival. As part of the festival's 2021 edition, maestro Daniel Harding leads the Orchestra del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino in a wonderful concert program featuring Johannes Brahms's Tragic Overture in D minor, Op. 81, and Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 1 in D major, ‘Titan'. Brahms composed his concert overture during the summer of 1880. With its dark and tumultuous character, it could be regarded as the serious counterpart of his more joyful Academic Festival Overture, Op. 80. Mahler completed his first symphony eight years later, in 1888. Originally a five-movement piece, the composer discarded the second movement ‘Blumine' some years later, resulting in the final four-movement version we know today. This performance was recorded at Teatro del Maggio Musicale Fiorentino in Florence, Italy, in 2021.

programme.title: Stephanie Childress, Alina Ibragimova & London Symphony Orchestra
programme.desc: Stephanie Childress conducts the London Symphony Orchestra and Sibelius and Mendelssohn provide the soundtrack for this unique concert with a distinctly Northern European flavor, live from London's LSO St Luke's. The evening begins with Jean Sibelius's En Saga, a symphonic poem that, like its composer, has become a defining figure of Finnish national musical identity. On the other side of the Baltic, it took Mendelssohn six years to complete his famed Violin Concerto—and it was well worth the wait, as violin virtuoso Alina Ibragimova's powerhouse rendition will demonstrate! The concert ends in style with two short works from Finland: Lumière et pesanteur by the fabulous Kaija Saariaho—ranked the world's greatest living composer by BBC Music Magazine—and another work by the endlessly inventive Sibelius, the spirited and episodic Cassazione.

programme.title: London Symphony Orchestra & Michael Tilson Thomas
programme.desc: American conductor Michael Tilson Thomas leads the London Symphony Orchestra (LSO) in an energetic performance of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's folk-infused Symphony No. 2 in C minor, Op. 17. Written in 1872, the work is nicknamed 'Little Russian' as the composer used several folk tunes from Ukraine, then known as ‘Little Russia'. This performance was recorded at LSO St Luke's, London, on May 31, 2021.

programme.title: Grunge
programme.desc: Grunge is very loud, very rebellious lots of very heavy guitar and fuzz effects, but in terms of its recording techniques, it's very lo-fi everything is stripped back, and really taken back to just the instruments. The lyrics tended to be about real life stuff, alienation a kind of weariness at the world a cynicism, but also a lot of humour. Grunge was the music of the underdog. There was no point aping glamour because there was no glamour in their lives, and it wasn't something that they could graduate to. So they would sit at home, play riffs, deep, big, huge riffs.

programme.title: Alt Rock
programme.desc: Emerging from the independent music underground of the 1970s it became widely popular in the 1980s. The music was unified by collective debt to the musical style and/or the independent, DIY ethos of punk rock, which laid the groundwork for alternative music. Throughout the 1980s, magazines, college radio airplay, and word of mouth had increased the prominence and highlighted the diversity of alternative rock, and with the breakthrough of Nirvana, grunge and Britpop movements in the 1990s, it entered the mainstream. programme.desc: Enjoy these Tropical Fish blended with Island Music and Calming Ocean Sounds! Perfect for Relaxing, Studying, Sleeping, Meditating, Date Night, Dinner Parties and More.

programme.title: Relaxing Jellyfish With Music & Ocean Sounds
programme.desc: Enjoy these Relaxing Jellyfish blended with Calming Music and Ambient Ocean Sounds! Perfect for Relaxing, Studying, Sleeping, Meditating, Date Night, Dinner Parties and More.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes
programme.desc: Beneath the Sea a compilation of spectacular underwater images from around the world. Captured in exclusively in 8K digital, Beneath the Sea features an award-winning original score by Alan Williams.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Turtle Paradise 2
programme.desc: Leave your worries from the world above behind and dive into a vibrant undersea world inhabited by the iconic sea turtles, for 2 full hours of Nature Relaxation. Captured by filmmaker collaborators Tilo & Yvonne Kühnast, Turtle Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time video scenes captured from around the Caribbean, paired with gentle underwater sounds & calming music for deep relaxation by Jason Stephenson.

programme.title: A Day on the Napali Coast 1
programme.desc: A 5 hour long ambient nature film featuring the spectacular sights and sounds of the legendary 11-mile long Kalalau Trail. Featuring hundreds of breathtaking yet tranquil scenes with stereo audio, this video begins on Kalalau Beach at sunrise and progresses along the trail over the course of nearly a full day. Viewers will enjoy a countless array of views that overlook the vast blue ocean, where an abundance of wildlife and nature appear in their pristine and healing majesty.

programme.title: Algarve: Portugal
programme.desc: Relax along the coastal cliffs in Portugal with this stunning 4K ambient nature film showcasing the stunning sandstone cliffs, glistening blue waters, peaceful waves and birds flying. Filmed by collaborator Nick de-Jonge Skeyes, it is presented without any music for a true immersion in nature.
